_meta.purpose: STATIC map-connection PRIORS for the keeper router (PASS 3 NS#40). The learned world graph only knows maps she has VISITED, so a keeper HOST reached by a door from an as-yet-unwalked corridor (Diglett's Cave off Route 11 / Route 2) is invisible to world.route -> the cross-map router returns None forever -> she never detours there -> she never visits it (the NS#39 chicken-and-egg). This table gives the router the STATIC knowledge 'area X is entered from overworld map(s) Y', so it can plot a bounded path to a reachable GATEWAY, ride there via the LEARNED graph, then step through the live-read door into the host and catch. Only INTERIOR/cave keeper hosts need this - overworld route hosts (Route 24/25 Abra, Route 7/8 Growlithe) are already reachable via learned map edges once an adjacent map is visited.
_meta.portable_engine_split: This is the swappable FireRed game-knowledge layer (rule 14). host_gateways is keyed by the SAME human place-name the encounters KB + _PLACE_NAMES use, so the engine stays game-agnostic. A new game supplies its own file; the router code is unchanged.
_meta.scope: ONLY caves/interiors that host a PLANNED coverage keeper AND are entered by a door (not a map edge). Diglett's Cave (Dugtrio, the ground slot) is the canonical + only currently-planned case. Gateways are the overworld maps whose map-header warps land in the area; the exact door TILE is discovered live via read_warps on arrival (source-first), so only the gateway map id is needed here.
_meta.map_ids: group,num per _PLACE_NAMES: Route N = (3, 18+N); Diglett's Cave = (1,36..38). So Route 2 = (3,20) [north mouth], Route 11 = (3,29) [south mouth].
